source_summary: Autoimmune disease overview plus example autoimmune diseases organized by body system.
medical_safety_note: This JSON is an accessibility/life-impact catalogue derived from the attached PDF and cautious general health-literacy reasoning. It is not a diagnostic tool, treatment plan, or medical advice. Individual impact varies, especially with comorbid conditions, environment, support, money, housing, work demands, flare cycles, and care access.

entry_schema.id: stable slug unique within this JSON
entry_schema.name: condition, limitation, concept, or mobility aid name
entry_schema.source_group: category used by the source PDF
entry_schema.source_page: first page where the source term appears, when found
entry_schema.source_excerpt: short extracted context from the PDF, when available
entry_schema.life_limitations: ways it may limit daily living, relationships, work, safety, mobility, communication, or energy
entry_schema.possible_life_improvements_or_supports: ways recognition, accommodations, treatment, tools, community, or the aid itself may slightly improve life/functioning
entry_schema.support_or_accommodation_ideas: non-prescriptive supports to discuss with appropriate professionals/support people
entry_schema.spoon_energy_notes: how it may affect planning under Spoon Theory / energy pacing
entry_schema.individual_variability_note: reminder that impact differs by person and day

source_specific_note: The PDF says autoimmune diseases can affect almost any tissue/organ and lists example diseases, not a complete list of all autoimmune diseases.

limits: Inflammation, pain, swelling, warmth/redness/discoloration, muscle weakness, joint stiffness, vision effects, high blood sugar, fatigue, and flares may affect daily life depending on condition.
Because symptoms can recur as flares/attacks, planning may require flexibility and recovery time.
possible_improvements_or_supports: Treatment cannot necessarily cure autoimmune disease, but symptom management, remission periods, physical/occupational therapy, medication, diet changes for specific conditions, and pacing can reduce daily disruption.

entries.name: Rheumatoid arthritis (RA)
entries.source_excerpt: Joints and muscles Rheumatoid arthritis (RA). Lupus. Myositis. Skin and blood vessels Sjögren’s syndrome. Psoriasis. Psoriatic arthritis. Dermatomyositis. Scleroderma. Vasculitis. Rheumatoid vasculitis. Urticarial vasculitis. Vitiligo. Digestive system Crohn’s disease. Celiac disease. Ulcerative colitis. Autoimmune gastritis. Endocrine system Type 1 diabetes. Addison’s disease. Hashimoto’s thyroiditis. Graves’ disease. Nervous system Multiple sclerosis (MS). Myasthenia gravi
life_limitations: Autoimmune disease can be chronic and may require lifelong symptom management, flare planning, appointments, and treatment decisions.
Symptoms may come and go, which can make work, chores, caregiving, social plans, and mobility less predictable.
Can limit walking, gripping, lifting, stairs, cooking, bathing, writing/typing, exercise, and sleep through pain, stiffness, swelling, muscle weakness, or fatigue.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: A clear diagnosis can support treatment, flare tracking, accommodations, and self-advocacy.
Recognizing triggers and early warning signs can slightly improve pacing and reduce preventable crashes.
Physical/occupational therapy, adaptive tools, movement pacing, and inflammation treatment may improve safety and independence.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoons may fluctuate with inflammation, pain, fatigue, treatment side effects, infection risk, and flares.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
